text:
**Action taken:** Sent an email to `oncall@acme.io` with subject **Triage**.

**Why:** There *are* high-urgency triggered incidents — 5 of them — so I did NOT post to Slack. Instead I sent the email listing every such incident's id.

**Incident IDs:** PD-400, PD-401, PD-403, PD-411, PD-418

text: Good. I can see PagerDuty's `list_incidents` accepts `:status` and `:urgency` filters. Let me fetch the triggered/high incidents

text: There are 5 high-urgency triggered incidents. So I must send an email with their IDs. Let me prepare the body and send.

tool_calls.name: execute_lisp
tool_calls.input:
{"code":"(def incidents (pagerduty__list_incidents {:status "triggered" :urgency "high"}))
(def ids (map :id incidents))
(def body (str "High-urgency triggered incidents:\n" (clojure.string/join "\n" ids)))
(email__send_email {:to "oncall@acme.io" :subject "Triage" :body body})"}
